description_wiki: Freddie Lee Shuttlesworth (born Freddie Lee Robinson, March 18, 1922 – October 5, 2011) was an American Baptist minister and civil rights activist who led fights against segregation and other forms of racism, during the civil rights movement.

entity: Alabama Christian Movement for Human Rights
surface_forms: Alabama Christian Movement for Human Rights
description: alabama christian movement for human rights
description_wiki: The Alabama Christian Movement for Human Rights (ACMHR) was an American civil rights organization in Birmingham, Alabama, which coordinated boycotts and sponsored federal lawsuits aimed at dismantling segregation in Birmingham and Alabama during the civil rights movement.

description: city in and county seat of Jefferson County, Alabama, United States
description_wiki: Birmingham ( BUR-ming-ham) is a city in the north central region of Alabama, United States.

description: organization with the job to help the public in emergency situations like fire
description_wiki: A fire department (North American English) or fire brigade (Commonwealth English), also known as a fire company, fire authority, fire district, fire and rescue, fire force, or fire service in some areas, is an organization that provides fire prevention and fire suppression services as well as other rescue services.
